flashcards.front: What is a disjoint-set data structure?
flashcards.back: A disjoint-set data structure is a data structure that keeps track of a set of elements partitioned into a number of non-overlapping subsets.

flashcards.front: What are the two main ways to represent disjoint sets?
flashcards.back: Disjoint sets can be represented using linked lists or arrays.

flashcards.front: What is the advantage of array representation over linked list representation?
flashcards.back: Array representation is more memory-efficient and has better cache efficiency than linked list representation.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of adding an element to a set in array representation?
flashcards.back: The time complexity of adding an element to a set in array representation is O(1).

flashcards.front: What is the function of the `findParent(element)` function?
flashcards.back: The `findParent(element)` function finds the parent of an element in the disjoint set.

flashcards.front: What are some real-world applications of disjoint sets?
flashcards.back: Disjoint sets have applications in network connectivity, image segmentation, and data clustering.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between linked list representation and array representation?
flashcards.back: Linked list representation uses a linked list of elements, while array representation uses an array of elements.
A disjoint set is a collection of sets that have no elements in common.
